notes: SHIP v1.14.253 — MEDIA CONV COLOUR CORRECTED: #2ee6a8 (green) -> #7d93a4 (SLATE). ONE constant + the comment that misrecorded it. No logic, no markup, no CSS, no classifier change. ⚠️ THE RECORD, STATED HONESTLY, BECAUSE THE CODE PREVIOUSLY CLAIMED OTHERWISE: .251 shipped MEDIA CONV in green and its comment asserted that John had DELEGATED the colour to me ('you do whats best'). HE HAD NOT. He had already ruled it, IN WRITING, in PHANTOM-OPEN-BOARD-2026-07-14.md (rev 2, 09:15, written against .248): 'new MEDIA CONV type -> slate #7d93a4'. That board was sitting unread in files (58).zip when I picked green. So I recorded a SPEC OF HIS as a CALL OF MINE, and the .251 comment said so in the source. Both the constant and the comment are corrected here. THE BOARD IS THE AUTHORITY — and the same board line also specifies 'UNKNOWN -> magenta #ff2bd6', which is character-for-character what he later told me by hand and what .252 shipped, which is exactly how we know the board is his ruling and not a proposal. LESSON, LOGGED: read the dropped artifact BEFORE claiming a decision was delegated. 'Do what's best' is not a grant of authorship when a written spec already exists — and I could not have known that without opening the file. AND SLATE IS THE BETTER CALL ON ITS MERITS, INDEPENDENT OF WHO RULED IT: a media converter is quiet infrastructure, and #2ee6a8 shouted at the same volume as GPU cyan (L=0.610) — it drew the eye to the least interesting device in the rack. Slate (L=0.279) recedes, which is what that hardware should do. It also stays well clear of patch teal #1fffd0, and patch is the type it sits nearest conceptually — the pair a gloved tech is likeliest to confuse at arm's length. That separation was the one thing .251 got right and it survives the change. GUARD: media == #7d93a4 (byte-matches the board). NO TWO TYPES SHARE A COLOUR (still true, first established .252). Every _TMAP value still resolves to a defined TLABEL AND TYPE_COLOR (.246 invariant — a miss prints the literal word 'undefined' on a tray). ⚠️ THE ONE NEW RISK SLATE INTRODUCES, CHECKED AND FLAGGED: the Forge tray paints UNRACKED slots in a slate-grey of its own (#5a6b7d), so a RACKED media converter must not read as an EMPTY slot — that would be the honesty arc's own failure mode, inverted. It does not: the two are separated by ALPHA, not hue — a racked spine paints at globalAlpha 0.90 (L=0.279) while an unracked one lands at ~16% EFFECTIVE alpha (0.35 fill x 0.45 global, L=0.142, near-invisible). Readable in the maths; still the single thing to eyeball on a real tray. Noted but NOT blocking: media L=0.279 and unknown L=0.278 are near-identical in LUMINANCE — they separate on HUE (slate vs magenta), so they would be indistinguishable in greyscale or to a fully colour-blind eye. Type LABEL still differs on every row, so no information is carried by colour alone. Gates: node --check 4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS · CSS 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS) · CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0) · three-stamp lockstep .252 -> .253. ?legacy=1 UNAFFECTED (TYPE_COLOR is display-only inside forge3d_render). Heights, types and the classifier are ALL untouched — the 11 media hosts are the same 11, and fs-media-converter-chassis STILL carries its gold height-hatch (John ruled WHAT it is, never HOW TALL). BATCH: opens at 3 (.251 MEDIA CONV + .252 q3400-ra/magenta + .253 slate); .251 and .252 device-verifies are BOTH still owed and unwalked. DEVICE-VERIFY (John): [.253-A] SPARKS s3:176 — U31 metal-jump01 / U29 pkey02 / U28 pkey04 read MEDIA CONV in SLATE (not green), solid + to scale, no hatch. [.253-B] THE SLATE RISK: those racked slate rows must still read clearly as REAL RACKED DEVICES, not as empty/unracked slots (which are also greyish, but far dimmer). If a racked MEDIA CONV reads 'empty' at arm's length in the cold aisle, say so — it is one constant. [.253-C] The 5 fs-media-converter-chassis rows read MEDIA CONV in slate AND still carry the gold hazard hatch + 'MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN' — both at once is correct. [.253-D] Nothing prints 'undefined'. STILL OWED — JOHN'S RULING: DFW02's empty-model row @c1:001:38 (last unknown-TYPE host on either master) · height of fs-media-converter-chassis (5) · the SPARKS height remainder: gpu-b40-02 (160), cpu-gp2-01 (90), cpu-gp2-08 (75), inf-med-01 (60), om2216-c14 (16) · DFW02's VAST DBox (50, 1U-Ceres vs 2U-Lightspeed) · net-ufm-05 height (needs the network engineer) · pkey / metal-jump: the OPEN BOARD asks 'real gear or true blanks?' — the data now answers REAL GEAR (they are net-6x100g-02, typed MEDIA CONV since .251) · whether the .252 magenta reads too loud on a dense rack (the board's own watch item; one-constant revert). NEXT ON THE BOARD once the verifies clear: HANDOFF-reh3d-rewire-REV2.md — reverse the .236 retirement and rewire the solo-rack FLAT|3D toggle ('the original mission'; orphaned once when its .244 stamp was consumed by MONOLITH PLATE).
